## Toolkit Tools (12)

✓ **Get Crypto Account** (get-account)
  Type: http
  Description: Retrieves the user's Robinhood Crypto account: account number, status (active/deactivated/sell_only), and current buying power in USD. Use this to verify auth works, check account health before trading, or read available cash for sizing orders.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Get Crypto Holdings** (get-holdings)
  Type: http
  Description: List the user's crypto holdings (asset code, total quantity, quantity available for trading). Optionally filter to a single asset code (e.g., "BTC", "ETH"). Returns paginated results.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Get Trading Pairs** (get-trading-pairs)
  Type: http
  Description: List supported crypto trading pairs (e.g., BTC-USD, ETH-USD) with their min/max order sizes, asset/quote increments, and trade status (tradable / untradable / sellonly). Optionally filter to a single symbol.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Get Best Bid/Ask** (get-best-bid-ask)
  Type: http
  Description: Get the current best bid and ask price (with spread, inclusive of Robinhood's buy/sell spread) for a single crypto symbol. Use this to read live market price before placing an order, or to compare Robinhood's spread to other venues.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Get Estimated Execution Price** (get-estimated-price)
  Type: http
  Description: Get the estimated execution price for a specific quantity of a crypto symbol on the bid, ask, or both sides. Use this to estimate slippage / total cost before placing an order. Quantities passed as strings to preserve precision.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Place Market Order** (place-market-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Place a market order on Robinhood Crypto. Executes immediately at the best available price (subject to Robinhood's spread). For BTC-USD a round-trip costs ~1.7% per side to spread. Provide asset_quantity as a string. client_order_id must be a unique string per order (use a UUID or timestamp-based unique string).
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Place Limit Order** (place-limit-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Place a GTC (good-till-canceled) limit order on Robinhood Crypto. Order rests on the book until limit_price is touched. Provide either asset_quantity (amount of the asset) OR quote_amount (amount in quote currency, e.g., USD), not both. client_order_id must be unique per order.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Place Stop-Loss Order** (place-stop-loss-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Place a GTC stop-loss order on Robinhood Crypto. Triggers a market order when stop_price is reached. Useful for protecting downside on an open long, or stop-buying into a breakout. Provide asset_quantity OR quote_amount, not both.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Place Stop-Limit Order** (place-stop-limit-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Place a GTC stop-limit order on Robinhood Crypto. When stop_price is reached, places a limit order at limit_price. Combines a stop trigger with limit-price control. Provide asset_quantity OR quote_amount.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **List Orders** (get-orders)
  Type: http
  Description: List the user's Robinhood Crypto orders, optionally filtered by symbol, side, state, or type. Returns a paginated list with each order's id, state, executions, and average price.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Get Order Details** (get-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Get full details of a single Robinhood Crypto order by its order ID (UUID). Returns state, type, side, fill quantity, average price, and per-execution detail.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Cancel Order** (cancel-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Cancel an open Robinhood Crypto order by its order ID. Returns success status. Cannot cancel orders that are already filled, canceled, or failed.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

## Toolkit Tools (13)

✓ **Get Current Weather** (get_current_weather)
  Type: http
  Description: Retrieves current weather conditions for a specific location using latitude and longitude coordinates. Returns temperature, humidity, wind speed, weather description, visibility, and more. Use this when you have exact coordinates or after using the geocode tool.
  Requires: OPENWEATHER_API

✓ **Get 5-Day Weather Forecast** (get_weather_forecast)
  Type: http
  Description: Retrieves a 5-day weather forecast with data every 3 hours for a specific location. Returns up to 40 forecast timestamps including temperature, weather conditions, precipitation probability, and wind data. Perfect for planning activities or trips.
  Requires: OPENWEATHER_API

✓ **Geocode City Name** (geocode_location)
  Type: http
  Description: Converts a city name, state, and/or country into geographic coordinates (latitude and longitude). Use this tool first when you only have a city name and need coordinates for weather lookups. Returns up to 5 matching locations.
  Requires: OPENWEATHER_API

✓ **Geocode ZIP/Postal Code** (geocode_zip)
  Type: http
  Description: Converts a ZIP code or postal code into geographic coordinates. Useful when users provide postal codes instead of city names. Works with US ZIP codes and postal codes from other countries.
  Requires: OPENWEATHER_API

✓ **Reverse Geocode Coordinates** (reverse_geocode)
  Type: http
  Description: Converts geographic coordinates (latitude/longitude) into location names. Useful when you have coordinates from GPS or maps and need to identify the city/area name.
  Requires: OPENWEATHER_API

✓ **Get Air Pollution Data** (get_air_pollution)
  Type: http
  Description: Retrieves current air pollution data including Air Quality Index (AQI) and concentrations of pollutants like CO, NO, NO₂, O₃, SO₂, PM2.5, PM10, and NH₃. AQI ranges from 1 (Good) to 5 (Very Poor). Use this when users ask about air quality, smog, or pollution levels.
  Requires: OPENWEATHER_API

✓ **Get Air Pollution Forecast** (get_air_pollution_forecast)
  Type: http
  Description: Retrieves air pollution forecast data for the upcoming days. Returns hourly AQI predictions and pollutant concentrations. Use this when users want to know future air quality for planning outdoor activities.
  Requires: OPENWEATHER_API

✓ **One Call API 3.0 Weather** (get_one_call_weather)
  Type: http
  Description: Retrieves comprehensive weather data using the One Call API 3.0 - the most advanced OpenWeatherMap endpoint. Returns current conditions, minute-by-minute forecast for 1 hour, hourly forecast for 48 hours, daily forecast for 8 days, and government weather alerts. Updated every 10 minutes. **REQUIRES: One Call by Call subscription (1,000 free calls/day after subscribing).**
  Requires: OPENWEATHER_API

✓ **AI Weather Overview** (get_weather_overview)
  Type: http
  Description: Retrieves a human-readable AI-generated weather summary for today and tomorrow. Uses OpenWeather's AI technology to provide natural language descriptions of weather conditions, perfect for conversational responses. **REQUIRES: One Call by Call subscription.**
  Requires: OPENWEATHER_API

✓ **AI Weather Assistant** (ask_weather_assistant)
  Type: http
  Description: Asks OpenWeather's AI Weather Assistant a natural language question about weather. Supports questions like "What should I wear in London tomorrow?" or "Will it rain in Paris this weekend?". The assistant understands 50+ languages and provides weather-related advice in a human-friendly format. **REQUIRES: One Call by Call subscription.**
  Requires: OPENWEATHER_API

✓ **Historical Weather (Timemachine)** (get_historical_weather)
  Type: http
  Description: Retrieves historical weather data for any specific moment in time using Unix timestamp. Access weather data from January 1st, 1979 to 4 days into the future. Perfect for analyzing past weather events, comparing weather patterns, or researching historical conditions. **REQUIRES: One Call by Call subscription.**
  Requires: OPENWEATHER_API

✓ **Daily Weather Summary** (get_daily_summary)
  Type: http
  Description: Retrieves aggregated daily weather statistics for a specific date. Returns min/max/afternoon/night/evening/morning temperatures, precipitation totals, humidity, pressure, wind data, and cloud coverage. Data available from January 2nd, 1979 up to 1.5 years into the future. **REQUIRES: One Call by Call subscription.**
  Requires: OPENWEATHER_API

✓ **Historical Air Pollution** (get_air_pollution_history)
  Type: http
  Description: Retrieves historical air pollution data for a specific time range. Returns hourly AQI (Air Quality Index) and pollutant concentrations (CO, NO, NO₂, O₃, SO₂, PM2.5, PM10, NH₃) for any period since November 27, 2020. Perfect for analyzing air quality trends. **Note: May require a paid subscription for historical access.**
  Requires: OPENWEATHER_API

## Toolkit Tools (25)

✓ **Get My Channel** (get-channel)
  Type: http
  Description: Get the authenticated user's YouTube channel info: id, title, description, customUrl, subscriber/video/view counts, branding settings, and the uploads playlist ID. The uploads playlist ID (items[0].contentDetails.relatedPlaylists.uploads) is what you pass to list-playlist-items to walk all of the user's uploaded videos.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Channel Branding** (get-channel-branding)
  Type: http
  Description: Get the authenticated channel's branding settings only (banner image, default language, country, channel keywords, watermark).
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **List My Videos** (list-videos)
  Type: http
  Description: List videos uploaded by the authenticated user via search.list with forMine=true. Returns lightweight search-result items (id.videoId + snippet). Call get-video on a specific videoId for full statistics + contentDetails. Note: search.list costs 100 quota units per call vs 1 for playlistItems.list — for heavy usage prefer chaining get-channel → list-playlist-items with the uploads playlist ID.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Video** (get-video)
  Type: http
  Description: Get detailed info about a YouTube video including title, description, tags, statistics (views/likes/comments), status (privacy, embeddable), and contentDetails (duration, definition, caption availability).
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Update Video** (update-video)
  Type: http
  Description: Update a video's metadata: title, description, tags, category, privacy, made_for_kids. WARNING: YouTube's videos.update replaces whole snippet/status blocks - if you omit a field that exists, it will be cleared. Call get-video first if you only want to change one field, and pass the existing values through for the rest.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Delete Video** (delete-video)
  Type: http
  Description: Delete a YouTube video. Permanent and cannot be undone.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **List Captions** (list-captions)
  Type: http
  Description: List caption tracks for a video. Returns each caption's id, language, name, trackKind (standard or ASR-auto), isCC, isDraft, status. Use the returned caption id with download-caption-srt to fetch the actual subtitle text.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Download Caption (SRT)** (download-caption-srt)
  Type: http
  Description: Download the actual subtitle text for a caption track in SRT format. Returns raw SRT (numbered cues + timestamps + text lines). For an existing video, first call list-captions to find the caption_id. Note: YouTube only lets you download captions you own (uploaded by you OR for videos you own). For other videos' transcripts use a public transcript scraper, not the API.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Update Caption Metadata** (update-caption)
  Type: http
  Description: Update caption track metadata (isDraft flag). Note: this toolkit's update-caption is metadata-only because changing the actual SRT/VTT body requires a multipart upload that HTTP tools cannot do natively. To replace caption content, delete and re-upload via the official YouTube site or a multipart-capable client.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Delete Caption** (delete-caption)
  Type: http
  Description: Delete a caption track. Permanent.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **List Playlists** (list-playlists)
  Type: http
  Description: List the authenticated user's playlists with snippet, status, and itemCount.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Create Playlist** (create-playlist)
  Type: http
  Description: Create a new playlist on the authenticated user's channel.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Update Playlist** (update-playlist)
  Type: http
  Description: Update a playlist's title, description, or privacy. WARNING: Like videos.update, this replaces the whole snippet/status; pass existing values for fields you don't want to clear.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Delete Playlist** (delete-playlist)
  Type: http
  Description: Delete a playlist. Permanent.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **List Playlist Items** (list-playlist-items)
  Type: http
  Description: List videos in a playlist. To list all videos uploaded by a channel, pass the channel's uploads playlist ID (get it from get-channel response items[0].contentDetails.relatedPlaylists.uploads).
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Add Video to Playlist** (add-to-playlist)
  Type: http
  Description: Add a video to a playlist. Returns the new playlist item (with playlist_item_id needed for remove-from-playlist).
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Remove from Playlist** (remove-from-playlist)
  Type: http
  Description: Remove a video from a playlist. playlist_item_id is the id of the playlist-item record (from list-playlist-items.items[].id), NOT the video_id.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **List Video Comments** (list-video-comments)
  Type: http
  Description: List comment threads (top-level comments and their replies) on a video.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Reply to Comment** (reply-to-comment)
  Type: http
  Description: Reply to a YouTube comment. parent_id is the top-level comment's id (from list-video-comments items[].snippet.topLevelComment.id), or another reply's id.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Delete Comment** (delete-comment)
  Type: http
  Description: Delete a comment (only your own comments or comments on videos you own).
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Moderate Comment** (moderate-comment)
  Type: http
  Description: Set the moderation status of a comment on one of your videos. published = visible, rejected = hidden, heldForReview = pending manual review.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Video Analytics** (get-video-analytics)
  Type: http
  Description: Get YouTube Analytics for a specific video over a date range. Default metrics: views, estimatedMinutesWatched, averageViewDuration, averageViewPercentage, likes, dislikes, comments, shares, subscribersGained, subscribersLost. Dates as YYYY-MM-DD.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Channel Analytics** (get-channel-analytics)
  Type: http
  Description: Get YouTube Analytics across the whole authenticated channel over a date range. Default metrics: views, estimatedMinutesWatched, averageViewDuration, subscribersGained, subscribersLost, videosAddedToPlaylists, videosRemovedFromPlaylists. Dates as YYYY-MM-DD.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Search YouTube** (search-videos)
  Type: http
  Description: Search YouTube for videos, channels, or playlists by free-text query. Returns search-result items with id.{videoId,channelId,playlistId} + snippet. Note: each search call costs 100 quota units.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Video Categories** (get-video-categories)
  Type: http
  Description: Get the list of YouTube video category IDs and names for a region. Use the returned category id when calling update-video with a category_id.
  Requires: YOUTUBE_REFRESH_TOKEN

## Toolkit Tools (10)

✓ **List Greenhouse Jobs** (list-greenhouse-jobs)
  Type: http
  Description: Lists all open job postings at a company that uses Greenhouse as its ATS. Returns slim job records (id, title, location, departments, offices, updated_at, absolute_url) — NO full description. Use this for discovery and headline-level filtering. Then call get-greenhouse-job with the chosen job id to fetch the full HTML description. No API key required. Examples of Greenhouse customers: Anthropic, Life360, Vannevar Labs, Together AI, Natera, Inspiren, Campminder.
  No credentials required

✓ **List Lever Jobs** (list-lever-jobs)
  Type: http
  Description: Lists all open job postings at a company that uses Lever as its ATS. Returns full posting objects with title, hostedUrl, applyUrl, categories (location/team/commitment/department), workplaceType (remote/on-site/hybrid), descriptionPlain, createdAt, and lists. No API key required. IMPORTANT: pass the Lever slug exactly as it appears in jobs.lever.co/{slug} — including any dots (e.g., 'branch.gg' not 'branch'). Examples of Lever customers: PointClickCare (slug 'pointclickcare'), Branch (slug 'branch.gg'), Netflix (slug 'netflix').
  No credentials required

✓ **List Ashby Jobs** (list-ashby-jobs)
  Type: http
  Description: Lists all open job postings at a company that uses Ashby as its ATS. Returns title, location, department, employmentType, descriptionHtml, descriptionPlain, jobUrl, and publishedAt. No API key required - hits Ashby's public job board API. Use this when you know a company runs on Ashby (URLs like jobs.ashbyhq.com/{org}). Examples of Ashby customers: Notion, Vercel, Ramp, Linear.
  No credentials required

✓ **List Workable Jobs** (list-workable-jobs)
  Type: http
  Description: Lists all open job postings at a company that uses Workable as its ATS. Returns title, shortcode, location, employmentType, function, full description, and applyUrl. No API key required - hits Workable's public widget API. Use this when you know a company runs on Workable (URLs like apply.workable.com/{subdomain} or jobs hosted under their own domain via Workable).
  No credentials required

✓ **List SmartRecruiters Jobs** (list-smartrecruiters-jobs)
  Type: http
  Description: Lists all open job postings at a company that uses SmartRecruiters as its ATS. Returns id, name, refNumber, location, department, industry, function, typeOfEmployment, experienceLevel, and a postingUrl. No API key required - hits SmartRecruiters' public postings API. Use this when you know a company runs on SmartRecruiters (URLs like careers.smartrecruiters.com/{company} or jobs.smartrecruiters.com/{company}). Examples of SmartRecruiters customers: Bosch, Visa, Skechers.
  No credentials required

✓ **Search Adzuna Jobs** (search-adzuna)
  Type: http
  Description: Searches Adzuna's aggregated job index by free-text query, location, and filters. Returns jobs with title, company, location, salary_min/max, description, redirect_url, and created date. REQUIRES API credentials (ADZUNA_APP_ID + ADZUNA_APP_KEY). Get them free at developer.adzuna.com. Use this for broad aggregated discovery when you don't know which specific ATS hosts a role, especially for location- or salary-filtered queries. Adzuna pulls from Indeed, ZipRecruiter, corporate boards, and many smaller sources.
  Requires: ADZUNA_APP_ID, ADZUNA_APP_KEY

✓ **Search JSearch (Google for Jobs)** (search-jsearch)
  Type: http
  Description: Searches the Google for Jobs index via JSearch v3 (RapidAPI). Hits the /search-v2 endpoint which aggregates jobs from Indeed, LinkedIn, ZipRecruiter, Workday, and corporate ATSes with full descriptions, employer info, location, salary estimates, and apply links. REQUIRES a RapidAPI key with JSearch subscription (JSEARCH_RAPIDAPI_KEY). This is the highest-coverage paid search tool — one call effectively queries the whole open web for jobs. Use this when you want maximum breadth, when you don't know which ATS to target, or when you need recent-only filtering across all sources.
  Requires: JSEARCH_RAPIDAPI_KEY

✓ **Get Greenhouse Job** (get-greenhouse-job)
  Type: http
  Description: Fetches the full job posting at a Greenhouse customer by job id, including the full HTML description content, departments, offices, requisition id, and updated_at. Use this after list-greenhouse-jobs has returned a slim list and the agent has picked a specific job worth qualifying in depth. No API key required.
  No credentials required

✓ **Get Lever Job** (get-lever-job)
  Type: http
  Description: Fetches a single Lever job posting by its post id, including the full description content, lists (responsibilities, requirements, etc.), categories, additional info, and apply URL. Use this after list-lever-jobs to qualify a specific role. No API key required.
  No credentials required

✓ **Get Ashby Job** (get-ashby-job)
  Type: http
  Description: Fetches a single Ashby job posting by id, including full descriptionHtml, descriptionPlain, department, team, location, employmentType, isRemote, publishedAt, jobUrl, and applyUrl. Use this after list-ashby-jobs to qualify a specific role. No API key required.
  No credentials required

## Toolkit Tools (2)

✓ **Get Market News (Finnhub)** (get-market-news)
  Type: http
  Description: Latest market/financial news headlines from Finnhub, filterable by category. Categories: general, forex, crypto, merger. Returns array of news items with headline, summary, source, url, datetime. NOTE: response can be large (60+ KB) — call sparingly and use minId for pagination if needed. For a specific topic search, use search-news instead.
  Requires: FINNHUB_API_KEY

✓ **Search News (Marketaux)** (search-news)
  Type: http
  Description: Search financial news by keyword on Marketaux, sorted by published date (newest first). Use this for topic-specific scans (e.g., 'bitcoin', 'fed rates', 'AI chips'). ALWAYS pass published_after to avoid getting stale articles — Marketaux indexes years of history. Pass an ISO date like '2026-04-01' for last month, or compute a recent date.
  Requires: MARKETAUX_API_KEY

## Toolkit Tools (23)

✓ **List Linked Accounts** (list-accounts)
  Type: http
  Description: List all linked Schwab brokerage accounts. Returns each account's plain account number AND its hashed value. The hashValue is required for every other account-specific tool in this toolkit (positions, balances, orders, transactions). Always call this tool first to discover account hashes; never hard-code them.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Account Details** (get-account)
  Type: http
  Description: Get full detail for one Schwab account: balances, buying power, equity, day-trading flags, and (optionally) positions. Pass the account's hashValue from list-accounts as accountHash. Set fields=\"positions\" to include current holdings inline.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Single Quote** (get-quote)
  Type: http
  Description: Get a real-time quote for one stock or ETF symbol. Returns last price, bid/ask + sizes, day high/low/open/close, 52-week range, total volume, and fundamentals (P/E, EPS, dividend yield). Use this for a deep look at one symbol; use get-quotes for multiple symbols at once.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Multiple Quotes** (get-quotes)
  Type: http
  Description: Get real-time quotes for multiple symbols in a single call. Faster than calling get-quote in a loop. Pass symbols as a single comma-separated string (no spaces).
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Price History (OHLCV)** (get-price-history)
  Type: http
  Description: Get historical OHLCV candles for a symbol. Use periodType + period to set lookback window, frequencyType + frequency to set candle granularity. For intraday, use periodType=day with frequencyType=minute. For multi-month daily, use periodType=month with frequencyType=daily. Returns timestamps in epoch ms.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Option Chain** (get-option-chain)
  Type: http
  Description: Get the full options chain for an underlying symbol. Returns calls and/or puts grouped by expiration date and strike, including bid/ask, last, volume, open interest, and Greeks (delta, gamma, theta, vega, rho) plus implied volatility. Use strikeCount to limit how many strikes around ATM you receive. Use fromDate/toDate to limit expirations.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Market Movers** (get-market-movers)
  Type: http
  Description: Get top market movers (gainers, losers, most active) for a major index or exchange. Useful for daily market scans and watchlist generation.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Market Hours** (get-market-hours)
  Type: http
  Description: Get the trading hours (regular session, pre-market, post-market) for one or more markets on a given date. Use to confirm if markets are open before placing orders.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Search Instruments** (search-instruments)
  Type: http
  Description: Search for instruments (stocks, ETFs) by symbol or company description. Use to look up exact tickers or get fundamental data for an existing ticker. The projection field controls match type: symbol-search for prefix match on ticker, desc-search for company-name match, fundamental for fundamentals payload.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get All Account Details** (get-all-accounts)
  Type: http
  Description: Get detail for ALL linked Schwab accounts in one call (balances, buying power, equity). Optionally include positions for each. Use this for a portfolio overview across multiple accounts; use get-account for a single account by hash.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Positions** (get-positions)
  Type: http
  Description: Get current open positions for one Schwab account, with quantity, average cost, current value, and day P/L. Wraps get-account with fields=positions; the response includes balances too. Pass accountHash from list-accounts.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Transactions** (get-transactions)
  Type: http
  Description: Get transaction history (trades, dividends, deposits, withdrawals, journals) for one account over a date range. startDate and endDate must be ISO-8601 timestamps with timezone (e.g., 2026-04-01T00:00:00.000Z), not plain dates. Filter by type or symbol if needed.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Orders** (get-orders)
  Type: http
  Description: List recent orders for one account, with status, fills, prices, and execution legs. fromEnteredTime and toEnteredTime are required by Schwab and must be full ISO-8601 datetimes with timezone (e.g., 2026-03-01T00:00:00.000Z).
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Get Single Order** (get-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Get full details of one specific order by orderId, including status, fills, execution legs, and editable/cancelable flags. Use after place-* tools to confirm an order, or to inspect an order before replace-order or cancel-order.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Cancel Order** (cancel-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Cancel a working (unfilled) order. Only orders whose cancelable flag is true can be canceled — confirm with get-order first if uncertain. Returns 200 on success with no body.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Replace (Modify) Order** (replace-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Replace an existing order with new parameters. Schwab cancels the old order and creates a new one atomically. Only works on editable orders. The body must be a complete Schwab Order object — fetch the original with get-order first, modify the fields you want, then send the whole shape back. Returns the new order ID in the Location header.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Place Market Order** (place-market-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Place a MARKET order to buy or sell shares at the current market price. Executes immediately during the chosen session. Use orderLegCollection[0] for a single equity leg. Schwab returns 201 with the new orderId in the Location response header.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Place Limit Order** (place-limit-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Place a LIMIT order — buy or sell shares at the specified price or better. Order rests until filled, canceled, or the duration expires. price is the limit price as a string (e.g., \"100.00\"). For BUY, fills at price or lower; for SELL, fills at price or higher.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Place Stop Order** (place-stop-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Place a STOP order. When the market price reaches stopPrice, a market order is triggered. Used to limit losses or protect profits. Supports equity OR option legs (set instrument.assetType accordingly). For equity stops on a long position, use SELL with stopPrice below current price.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Place Stop-Limit Order** (place-stop-limit-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Place a STOP_LIMIT order. When the market reaches stopPrice, a LIMIT order at price is created. Gives more execution control than a stop order — but the limit order can fail to fill if the market gaps through it. Supports equity or option legs.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Place Trailing Stop Order** (place-trailing-stop-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Place a TRAILING_STOP order. The stop price trails the market by a fixed percentage or dollar offset. For SELL trailing stops on long positions, the stop ratchets UP as price rises and never moves down. Use to lock in profits while letting winners run. stopPriceLinkType=PERCENT means stopPriceOffset is a percentage (5 = 5%); stopPriceLinkType=VALUE means stopPriceOffset is a dollar amount.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Place Option Order** (place-option-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Place an order to buy or sell an option contract (calls or puts). Get a valid option symbol from get-option-chain (OCC format, 21 chars including padding, e.g., \"AAPL  250117C00200000\"). Use BUY_TO_OPEN to open a long, SELL_TO_CLOSE to close a long, SELL_TO_OPEN to write/sell short, BUY_TO_CLOSE to cover a short. price is the per-contract premium (1 contract = 100 shares). LIMIT is strongly recommended for options.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

✓ **Place Bracket Order (Entry + Stop + Target)** (place-bracket-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Place a bracket order: a parent BUY entry that, when filled, triggers a OCO pair containing a take-profit LIMIT and a stop-loss STOP. When one exit fills, the other auto-cancels. Uses orderStrategyType=TRIGGER for the parent and OCO for the child group. Set parent orderType=MARKET (omit price) for market entry, or LIMIT (with price) for limit entry. All three legs share symbol and quantity.
  Requires: SCHWAB_REFRESH_TOKEN

## Toolkit Tools (18)

✓ **Get Spot OHLCV Candles (Binance)** (get-spot-candles)
  Type: http
  Description: Fetch OHLCV candlestick data from Binance spot (most liquid crypto venue). Use this to compute trend, momentum, and volatility, or to feed candles into technical indicator calculations. Returns up to 1000 candles per call. Each candle is an array: [openTime_ms, open, high, low, close, volume, closeTime_ms, quoteAssetVolume, numberOfTrades, takerBuyBaseVolume, takerBuyQuoteVolume, ignore]. Prices and volumes are returned as strings to preserve precision.
  No credentials required

✓ **Get Spot Order Book Depth (Binance)** (get-spot-orderbook)
  Type: http
  Description: Fetch the live order book (bids and asks) from Binance spot for a given symbol. Use this to: (1) compute the true mid-price ((best_bid+best_ask)/2) on the most liquid venue, (2) estimate slippage at order size by walking the book, (3) compare against Robinhood's spread before placing an order. Each level is [price, quantity] as strings.
  No credentials required

✓ **Get Spot 24h Ticker (Binance)** (get-spot-ticker-24h)
  Type: http
  Description: Get 24-hour rolling-window price-change statistics for a symbol on Binance spot: priceChange, priceChangePercent, weightedAvgPrice, lastPrice, bidPrice, askPrice, highPrice, lowPrice, volume (base), quoteVolume. Use this to read current price and 24h move in one call before deciding whether to act.
  No credentials required

✓ **Get Perp Funding Rate History (OKX)** (get-funding-rate)
  Type: http
  Description: Get historical 8-hour funding-rate settlements for an OKX perpetual swap. Funding rate is a leading sentiment indicator: persistently positive funding = crowded longs (squeeze risk to the downside); persistently negative funding = crowded shorts (squeeze risk to the upside). Use to gauge derivatives positioning before placing a directional spot trade. Switched from Binance Futures (geo-blocked) to OKX. Symbol format: 'BTC-USDT-SWAP' (linear perp, USDT-margined). Returns array of {instId, fundingRate, realizedRate, fundingTime, ...}.
  No credentials required

✓ **Get Perp Mark Price (OKX)** (get-perp-mark-price)
  Type: http
  Description: Get the current mark price for an OKX perpetual swap. Mark price is the fair value used for liquidation/PnL calculations and is closer to the index than last-trade price. Useful as a sanity-check reference vs. spot. For funding info, call get-funding-rate. Switched from Binance Futures (geo-blocked).
  No credentials required

✓ **Get Open Interest & Volume History (OKX)** (get-open-interest-history)
  Type: http
  Description: Time-series of aggregated open interest (OI) and trading volume across all OKX contracts for a base currency. Rising OI with rising price = new longs (trend confirmation); rising OI with falling price = new shorts; falling OI = positions closing (potential exhaustion). Switched from Binance Futures (geo-blocked). Returns array of [timestamp_ms, open_interest_USD, volume_USD] tuples.
  No credentials required

✓ **Get Long/Short Account Ratio (OKX)** (get-long-short-ratio)
  Type: http
  Description: Time-series of the long/short account ratio across OKX contracts for a base currency. Ratio > 1 = more long accounts than short; < 1 = more short. Extreme readings often coincide with reversals (the crowd is wrong). Switched from Binance Futures (geo-blocked). Returns array of [timestamp_ms, longShortRatio] tuples.
  No credentials required

✓ **Get DVOL Implied Volatility Index (Deribit)** (get-deribit-volatility)
  Type: http
  Description: Deribit's DVOL is the crypto equivalent of the VIX: a 30-day forward-looking annualized implied volatility index derived from BTC or ETH options. High DVOL = options market expects big moves (defensive sizing warranted); low DVOL = expected calm. Returns OHLC of the DVOL index over the requested window. Resolution '1' = 1-min, '60' = 1-hr, '3600' = 1-hr (alt), '43200' = 12-hr, '1D' = daily.
  No credentials required

✓ **Get Crypto Fear & Greed Index (alternative.me)** (get-fear-and-greed)
  Type: http
  Description: The Crypto Fear & Greed Index, scaled 0-100. 0-24 = Extreme Fear (often a buy signal), 25-49 = Fear, 50-74 = Greed, 75-100 = Extreme Greed (often a sell/de-risk signal). Aggregates volatility, momentum, social, surveys, dominance, and Google Trends. Use limit=1 for the latest reading, or higher for trend (each entry is one day). No auth required. Returns {data: [{value, value_classification, timestamp, time_until_update}]}.
  No credentials required

✓ **Get Stablecoin Distribution by Chain (DefiLlama)** (get-stablecoin-flows)
  Type: http
  Description: Total stablecoin circulating supply broken down by blockchain. Spot which chains are seeing stablecoin growth (capital flowing in) vs. drain. Returns ~30 chains with totalCirculatingUSD, change_1d, change_7d. Smaller than the historical chart endpoint (which exceeds response size). No auth.
  No credentials required

✓ **Get Coin Prices Snapshot (CoinGecko)** (get-coin-prices)
  Type: http
  Description: Bulk price snapshot for up to ~30 coins in one call. Returns USD price, market cap, 24h volume, and 24h % change per coin. Use for watchlist refresh or multi-asset comparison without N separate calls. Replaces defi-categories (DefiLlama Pro-walled). Works without a key.
  No credentials required

✓ **Get Top Crypto by Volume (CoinGecko)** (get-top-by-volume)
  Type: http
  Description: Top 20 cryptocurrencies by 24h trading volume, with price, mcap, 24h % change, and high/low. Use to scan 'what's most active right now' — high-volume coins are where action is concentrated. Replaces the DeFi TVL history endpoint (response too large). Works without a key.
  Requires: COINGECKO_API_KEY

✓ **Get DeFi Protocol Current TVL (DefiLlama)** (get-protocol-tvl)
  Type: http
  Description: Current Total Value Locked for a DeFi protocol, as a single USD number. Use to gauge ecosystem health for a protocol's native token (e.g., AAVE token vs. Aave protocol TVL). Sustained TVL growth = rising real usage. For history, this lightweight endpoint returns just the latest snapshot. No auth.
  No credentials required

✓ **Get Coin Snapshot (CoinGecko)** (get-coin-snapshot)
  Type: http
  Description: Cross-venue weighted snapshot of a single coin: USD price, market cap, 24h volume, 24h/7d/30d % moves, all-time high/low and distance from them, supply metrics. CoinGecko aggregates 1700+ exchanges so the price is closer to a true global mid than any single venue. Use this BEFORE placing a Robinhood order to sanity-check that RH's quote isn't far off the global mid. Works on the free public tier without a key, with stricter rate limits; set COINGECKO_API_KEY to unlock the Demo tier (10k calls/month).
  Requires: COINGECKO_API_KEY

✓ **Get Global Crypto Market Overview (CoinGecko)** (get-market-overview)
  Type: http
  Description: Top-of-the-funnel market context: total crypto market cap (USD), 24h total volume, BTC dominance %, ETH dominance %, number of active cryptocurrencies and markets, and 24h % change of total mcap. Use to read the macro crypto environment in one call. Rising BTC dominance during a sell-off = flight to quality (alts get hit harder); falling dominance with rising mcap = altseason. Works without a key.
  Requires: COINGECKO_API_KEY

✓ **Get Top 24h Gainers (CoinGecko)** (get-top-gainers)
  Type: http
  Description: Top 15 cryptocurrencies by 24h % price change (descending). Use to scan 'what's pumping' — high-momentum names. Replaces the trending-coins endpoint which had bloated currency-translation responses. Works without a key.
  Requires: COINGECKO_API_KEY

✓ **Get Technical Indicator (TAAPI.io)** (get-indicator)
  Type: http
  Description: Compute a single technical indicator on a Binance crypto pair using TAAPI.io. Common usage: RSI(14) on 1h to gauge overbought/oversold (RSI > 70 = overbought, < 30 = oversold), ATR(14) on 1d to size stops, EMA(20)/EMA(50) crossover for trend. Requires TAAPI_SECRET. Pair format: 'BTC/USDT' (slash separator). Hardcoded to Binance data — if other exchanges needed, would require a tool variant.
  Requires: TAAPI_SECRET

✓ **Get Macro Time Series (FRED)** (get-fred-series)
  Type: http
  Description: Fetch any time series from FRED (Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis). Crypto correlates with several macro factors; the high-value series for crypto trading are: 'DGS10' (10-year Treasury yield, inverse to risk assets), 'DGS2' (2-year), 'T10YIE' (10-year inflation expectations), 'DTWEXBGS' (broad dollar index, inverse to BTC), 'SOFR' (overnight rate), 'VIXCLS' (equity volatility). Requires free FRED_API_KEY. Returns observations array of {date, value}.
  Requires: FRED_API_KEY

## Toolkit Tools (12)

✓ **Get Crypto Account** (get-account)
  Type: http
  Description: Retrieves the user's Robinhood Crypto account: account number, status (active/deactivated/sell_only), and current buying power in USD. Use this to verify auth works, check account health before trading, or read available cash for sizing orders.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Get Crypto Holdings** (get-holdings)
  Type: http
  Description: List the user's crypto holdings (asset code, total quantity, quantity available for trading). Optionally filter to a single asset code (e.g., "BTC", "ETH"). Returns paginated results.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Get Trading Pairs** (get-trading-pairs)
  Type: http
  Description: List supported crypto trading pairs (e.g., BTC-USD, ETH-USD) with their min/max order sizes, asset/quote increments, and trade status (tradable / untradable / sellonly). Optionally filter to a single symbol.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Get Best Bid/Ask** (get-best-bid-ask)
  Type: http
  Description: Get the current best bid and ask price (with spread, inclusive of Robinhood's buy/sell spread) for a single crypto symbol. Use this to read live market price before placing an order, or to compare Robinhood's spread to other venues.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Get Estimated Execution Price** (get-estimated-price)
  Type: http
  Description: Get the estimated execution price for a specific quantity of a crypto symbol on the bid, ask, or both sides. Use this to estimate slippage / total cost before placing an order. Quantities passed as strings to preserve precision.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Place Market Order** (place-market-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Place a market order on Robinhood Crypto. Executes immediately at the best available price (subject to Robinhood's spread). For BTC-USD a round-trip costs ~1.7% per side to spread. Provide asset_quantity as a string. client_order_id must be a unique string per order (use a UUID or timestamp-based unique string).
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Place Limit Order** (place-limit-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Place a GTC (good-till-canceled) limit order on Robinhood Crypto. Order rests on the book until limit_price is touched. Provide either asset_quantity (amount of the asset) OR quote_amount (amount in quote currency, e.g., USD), not both. client_order_id must be unique per order.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Place Stop-Loss Order** (place-stop-loss-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Place a GTC stop-loss order on Robinhood Crypto. Triggers a market order when stop_price is reached. Useful for protecting downside on an open long, or stop-buying into a breakout. Provide asset_quantity OR quote_amount, not both.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Place Stop-Limit Order** (place-stop-limit-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Place a GTC stop-limit order on Robinhood Crypto. When stop_price is reached, places a limit order at limit_price. Combines a stop trigger with limit-price control. Provide asset_quantity OR quote_amount.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **List Orders** (get-orders)
  Type: http
  Description: List the user's Robinhood Crypto orders, optionally filtered by symbol, side, state, or type. Returns a paginated list with each order's id, state, executions, and average price.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Get Order Details** (get-order)
  Type: http
  Description: Get full details of a single Robinhood Crypto order by its order ID (UUID). Returns state, type, side, fill quantity, average price, and per-execution detail.
  Requires: ROBINHOOD_API_KEY, ROBINHOOD_PRIVATE_KEY

✓ **Cancel Order** (cancel-order)
  Type: http
